title: Massassi (language)

'''Massassi''' was an ancient tongue spoken by the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]],<ref name="Wildlife">''[[The Wildlife of Star Wars: A Field Guide]]''</ref> the warrior caste of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]].<ref name="BoS">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>

This Massassi writing system used symbols, but one symbol did not always correspond to one letter in the [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] or [[High Galactic/Legends|High Galactic alphabets]]. This resulted in a wide range of time-consuming possibilities when translating. Notable texts in the language included the [[Books of Massassi]]. [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] had hieroglyphs in the Massassi tongue etched into the [[Color/Legends|obsidian]] walls of [[Temple of Exar Kun|his temple]].

[[Xenographologist]]s discovered that the [[Massassi (alphabet)|Massassi alphabet]] had ties to the simplified component of [[High Sith]] writing.<ref name="TWW" />

By [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Streen]] had completed translating sixteen tablets of the Books of Massassi. In [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], during their time at the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], Jedi trainees [[Anakin Solo]] and [[Tahiri Veila]] translated ancient Massassi symbols to learn how to break the curse of the [[Golden Globe]] which had been constructed by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] in order to imprison the children of the Massassi and drain their powers for his purposes.

==Phrases==
*''Mnnt Yttl Gythh. Wyyrl Tmmn Zyrth!'' - He is the one we have waited for... bring him to the [[Temple of Fire]]!<ref name="DLotS4" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:RMQ-TempleBlueleafCluster.jpg|thumb|Example of Massassi script from ''The Illustrated Star Wars Universe'']]

Visual examples of Massassi script can be seen in [[Ralph McQuarrie]]'s ''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''.<ref name="blog">{{Blog|JSarek|8|Endnotes for The Written Word}}</ref>
